page: Amazon Web Services i8g.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The system features 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It achieves top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, and Redis SET operations, which reached 349.81 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression fall into the average tier. Large-language model inference speeds for prompt processing are average for medium models and weak for small models.

Given its high resource density, massive local NVMe SSD capacity, and high memory bandwidth, the i8g.48xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads. The combination of 192 dedicated physical cores and 45 TB of local storage makes it highly suitable for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, data warehousing, and intensive static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of RAM, 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by its high scores in Redis SET operations and Clang software compilation. However, single-core performance and LLM inference speeds remain average to weak. This server provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for high-density storage and memory-intensive deployments. It is best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput caching layers, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 192 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 1536 GB RAM, 45,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 192 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density ARMv9 storage-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated cores, 1.5 TB of RAM, and 45 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM and 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Achieves top-tier performance in database operations and memory bandwidth
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and high-throughput storage workloads
